skill.name: dead-code-removal
skill.description: Detects unused imports, functions, and classes across common languages, then guides conservative removal with backups and validation.
skill.summary: Finds dead code and guides safe cleanup with dependency checks, backups, and validation.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found most are Markdown examples or safety rules rather than executable malicious logic. The skill still carries medium risk because it directs agents to modify source files, create repository backups, and run local validation commands.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution During Cleanup Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run helper, lint, build, and test commands. This is expected for code cleanup, but it can execute project-defined scripts and should be reviewed in untrusted repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and are part of the intended workflow. The risk is contextual because the commands are standard development commands, not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Repository Backup May Duplicate Sensitive Files
medium_findings.description: The backup example copies the current working tree to a local backup directory. This can duplicate secrets, build artifacts, or large directories if users run it without exclusions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem-copy command is directly shown in the skill instructions. It is a legitimate safety mechanism, but it can create local exposure or excessive storage use.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Code Terms Are Safety Examples
low_findings.description: The eval and dynamic import references tell the agent not to remove code when dynamic usage is detected. They are not executable code in the skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear under a Never Remove If section and describe preservation checks. No evidence found that the skill evaluates user-controlled code.

low_findings.title: Static Keyword Matches Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and network reconnaissance alerts are keyword matches in descriptive Markdown and output examples. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, scanning, or network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe the skill, helper scripts, or validation report text. They do not contain network reconnaissance behavior or weak cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Backup Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The backup example uses command substitution to create a timestamped directory name. This is common shell usage, but it is still shell execution in user workflows.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell pattern is present and intentional. It appears benign, but users should review it before running in sensitive worktrees.

dangerous_patterns.title: Project Test and Build Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends running language-specific validation commands. These commands may trigger repository scripts, so users should inspect projects before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and standard for development. Their risk depends on the repository being analyzed, especially package scripts and build hooks.

content.value_statement: Unused code increases maintenance cost and hides defects. This skill guides conservative cleanup with dependency checks, backups, and validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Identifies likely unused imports, functions, and classes across common project structures.
Maps entry points, dependency paths, framework hooks, and test files before removal.
Uses a Python AST helper for unused import analysis in Python files.
Preserves code with dynamic usage patterns, framework registrations, or public API signals.
Guides incremental removals with backups, syntax checks, tests, and rollback steps.
Reports removed and preserved items with reasons and impact metrics.
limitations: Cannot prove runtime reachability for all dynamic language patterns.
Requires project tests or build commands for reliable validation.
May preserve code that appears unused but is loaded by configuration or reflection.
May require human review before removing exported APIs or generated files.

use_cases.title: Clean Refactored Services
use_cases.description: Find unused imports and helper functions after service refactoring, then remove only items validated by tests.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Branches
use_cases.description: Review stale code before deployment to reduce maintenance burden and avoid shipping unused paths.

use_cases.title: Modernize Legacy Modules
use_cases.description: Audit older modules for abandoned functions while preserving framework hooks, migrations, and public interfaces.

prompt_templates.title: Find Unused Imports
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner cleanup in a small Python module
prompt_templates.prompt: Find unused imports in the current Python files. Report what is safe to remove before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Clean One Directory
prompt_templates.scenario: Conservative cleanup in one source folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze src/ for unused imports and functions. Preserve tests, framework hooks, dynamic references, and exported APIs.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Validation Pass
prompt_templates.prompt: After this refactor, identify dead code, remove it incrementally, and run the available syntax checks and tests after each step.

prompt_templates.title: Full Audit With Risk Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the repository for dead code. Build an entry point map, explain preserved items, create a backup plan, and propose validated removals.

output_examples.input: Clean unused imports in a utility module.
output_examples.output: The skill reports total imports, names the unused imports, explains why each is safe, and confirms syntax and tests after removal.

output_examples.input: Find unused service functions after a feature removal.
output_examples.output: The skill separates removable functions from preserved framework or route references, then summarizes removed lines and validation status.

output_examples.input: Audit a release branch for dead code.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a cleanup report with analyzed areas, preserved entry points, recommended removals, and commands used for validation.

best_practices: Run dead code removal on a clean branch with version control available.
Review every proposed removal before applying changes to exported or framework-managed code.
Run syntax checks, tests, and builds after each small cleanup batch.
anti_patterns: Do not remove code only because it lacks direct text references.
Do not run project scripts from an untrusted repository without inspection.
Do not clean generated files, migrations, or public APIs without owner approval.

faq.question: Does this skill delete code automatically?
faq.answer: It guides incremental removals and validation. Users should approve risky changes before they are applied.

faq.question: Which languages are covered?
faq.answer: The guidance covers Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Go, and Rust project patterns.

faq.question: Can it detect dynamic runtime usage?
faq.answer: It checks common dynamic patterns, but runtime behavior can still require human review.

faq.question: Why does it create backups?
faq.answer: Backups support rollback when cleanup breaks tests or removes code that is still needed.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill recommends running local commands, and project scripts can execute arbitrary repository code.

faq.question: Will it remove tests or migrations?
faq.answer: The skill explicitly preserves test files, migrations, framework hooks, and other high-risk patterns.
